questions.question: You touch down at 72 KIAS, and come to a complete stop in 9 seconds. If the field elevation is 4,216', altimeter setting is 29.67" Hg, and temperature is 62°F, what was your rate of deceleration after touchdown?
Your rotation speed is 76 knots, and it takes 11 seconds to reach rotation speed on takeoff with no wind. What is your acceleration rate?

Referencing the previous question, if you applied brakes at 98 knots with 4,300' of runway remaining, and released the brakes 7 seconds later at 20 knots with 1,100' of runway remaining, what was your rate of deceleration?
questions.context: Referenced question 5: Your flight was 129 NM on a true course of 178°. You use your GPS to track directly to the destination and fly at 137 KTAS. If your MH is 169° in cruise and the flight takes 62 minutes, what are the winds aloft? [[NEXT_LINE]] Variation=5E.

questions.question: Referencing the previous question, if the Swift lifts off at 70 MPH, what is its average rate of acceleration during the takeoff roll?
questions.context: Referenced question 34: With the applicable FAA approvals taken care of, you are using your camera drone to take a video of your friend's takeoff in her newly restored Globe Swift. The Swift has a takeoff roll of 970', and it takes 11 seconds to reach liftoff speed. Your drone has a maximum speed of 35 MPH. You will fly the drone parallel to the runway at maximum speed to capture the takeoff. If you want the Swift to lift off exactly when it is abeam the drone, how long should your friend wait after the drone passes to begin her takeoff roll?

questions.question: You begin your takeoff roll from a stop on runway 31, and lift off at 68 KTAS 9 seconds later. Wind is 350@14 knots. What was your rate of ground speed acceleration during the takeoff roll?
